title: 5G SA Standalone Network Management Tools: Complete Technical Review for 2026
description: Looking for the best 5G SA management tools? We review tools for SA core network management, access network management, and slice management. Compare features, pricing, and see detailed recommendations for your telecom operations!
content:
5G SA Standalone Network Management Tools: Complete Technical Review for 2026

5G SA (standalone) is the complete 5G architecture with independent 5G core and access networks. Managing SA networks requires specialized tools for core network management, access network management, and slice management. We're reviewing the top 5G SA management tools to help operators pick the right technical solutions.

Understanding 5G SA Architecture

5G SA has specific characteristics: independent 5G core network without relying on 4G, independent 5G access network without 4G assistance, end-to-end 5G capabilities, and network slice support for slice capabilities. Each factor shapes your management needs.

Managing 5G SA involves key tasks: managing 5G core network elements like AMF, SMF, and UPF, managing 5G access network elements like gNodeB, managing network slices by creating and adjusting them, and monitoring SA network performance. Good tools handle all of these.

Top SA Management Tools

1. Nokia 5G SA Management

Nokia's SA management tool provides telecom-grade capabilities. It manages 5G core network elements including AMF, SMF, and UPF, manages 5G access network elements including gNodeB, manages network slices by creating and adjusting them, and monitors SA network performance.

Nokia SA's strength is SA slice management depth. Refined SA slice management creates and adjusts slices. Core network management handles everything comprehensively. Access network management covers all elements. Performance monitoring measures precisely. Integration with Nokia 5G SA equipment ensures accuracy. Cloud management handles distributed SA.

2. Ericsson 5G SA Management

Ericsson's SA management provides telecom capabilities. It manages 5G core network elements, manages 5G access network elements, manages network slices, and monitors SA network performance.

Ericsson SA's advantage is SA core network management depth. Comprehensive SA core network management handles all core network elements. Access network management manages elements. Slice management handles slices. Performance monitoring measures performance. Integration with Ericsson 5G SA equipment guarantees accuracy. Cloud management supports remote SA.

3. Huawei 5G SA Management

Huawei's SA management tool provides telecom capabilities. It manages 5G core network elements, manages 5G access network elements, manages network slices, and monitors SA network performance. It's adapted for domestic operator requirements.

Huawei SA stands out with localization support. Deep adaptation to domestic operators' 5G SA management processes and regulations. Flexible customization lets operators adjust functions. Strong technical support provides local services. Integration with Huawei 5G SA equipment ensures accuracy. AI SA management reduces manual work.

4. ZTE 5G SA Management

ZTE's SA management provides telecom capabilities. It manages 5G core network elements, manages 5G access network elements, manages network slices, and monitors SA network performance.

ZTE SA's advantage is cost-effectiveness. Compared to international brands, it offers more competitive pricing. Core network management handles elements. Access network management manages elements. Slice management handles slices. Performance monitoring measures performance. Localization support meets domestic operator requirements.

What's Coming in 2026

5G SA management is moving toward intelligence, automation, and slice refinement. AI becomes central - intelligently managing SA networks, optimizing slices, monitoring performance. Automated SA management cuts manual work and boosts efficiency. Refined slice management adjusts slices carefully for business requirements.

SA slice automation will be a priority. Tools need to automatically create and adjust slices, cutting manual configuration. SA automation creates a complete loop of core network management, access network management, slice management, and performance monitoring. SA expansion supports more SA scenarios.

2026 is a critical year for 5G SA intelligent transformation. Mature AI technology brings smarter management. Automation reduces manual workload. Refined slicing meets business requirements. Operators should choose SA management tools with AI management, automated operations, and refined slicing to prepare for 5G SA management.
